title: lichen_make_ula only checks first byte of prefix
description:
ipv6_addr.c:116: lichen_make_ula() checks 'prefix[0] != 0xfd'.

ULA range is fd00::/8, meaning the first byte must be exactly 0xfd. This check is correct.

However, the function name and doc say it 'Combines a fd00::/8 prefix with the IID'. The check only validates the first byte. A malformed prefix like fd00:ffff:ffff:ffff with the wrong bits in bytes 1-7 would pass.

Actually, bytes 1-7 are the L+Global ID per RFC 4193 - they can be any value. The check is correct.

Cancel - not a bug.
